name: Ferde Grofé
description: American composer, arranger, pianist and instrumentalist (1892–1972)
abstract: Ferdinand Rudolph von Grofé (March 27, 1892 –  April 3, 1972), known as Ferde Grofé () was an American composer, arranger, pianist, and instrumentalist. He is best known for his 1931 five-movement symphonic poem the Grand Canyon Suite, and for orchestrating George Gershwin's Rhapsody in Blue for its 1924 premiere.

works_created.t: Tin Pan Alley: The Melodic Decades
